title: Pilosella tabergensis
intro: Pilosella tabergensis, also known as Taberg saxifrage, is a rare species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella tabergensis include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pilosella tabergensis is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pilosella tabergensis is classified within the family Asteraceae, specifically within the subfamily Cichorioideae. This species is further categorized as a member of the genus Pilosella, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. The Pilosella genus is closely related to the Taraxacum genus, with some species exhibiting similar characteristics. Pilosella tabergensis is native to Sweden and is often found growing in rocky and mountainous regions. It is considered to be an alpine plant, thriving in areas with limited vegetation and harsh weather conditions.
